title: Promote Atelier to the main filling panel with CKC, Posekit, and Ingest sibling tabs
runtime_proof: test_ckc_embed 17/17 pass (ac6 Argus steer of full-window Atelier; negative-chrome; R1 chrome-reappears; R2 MAIN keeps chrome; module-ingest->Ingest landing). Pre-existing baseline failures NOT caused/included: test_accesskit_ids (KERNEL-011 4-pane), test_module_switcher switcher_paints (dual-Atelier label), test_atelier_panel posekit preview:// local (MT-014 scaffolding).
operator_request: Atelier should now be a main window filling panel. Castkit Codex should be a subtab of that panel. Posekit is a sibling of Castkit Codex and can run in a tab next to CKC. Ingest is also a tab. Everything should be usable by parallel agents inside Handshake and visually inspectable.

remediation.reason: MT-040 audit found MT-006 achieved a filling PANE but not a clean full-window MODULE: Atelier still renders with PaneHeader + outer pane TabBar chrome plus a second inner CKC/PoseKit/Ingest tab strip (double tab bar). Operator directive: redo MT-006 in parts.
parts: P1: full-window module render / chrome-suppression for Atelier (no PaneHeader, no outer pane TabBar); pane path untouched for Notes/others.
P2: single coherent CKC/INGEST/PoseKit sub-module tab bar (remove KernelDcc/other sibling pane tabs from the Atelier module surface).
P3: reconcile module-ckc/module-ingest into one coherent Atelier module (preserve stable ids module-ckc/module-ingest).
open_design_decisions: D3 full-window marker: module-level flag vs pane render mode
remediation.argus_mandate: Argus is the mandatory visual driver; inspect the full-window Atelier panel/tabs (and menu bar for MT-041) via Argus; expand Argus if it cannot see/steer a surface (ARGUS-009/010/011, HBR-VIS-005, CX-503D1).

finalized_approach.predicate: full_window_module = active_module.definition().full_window (true for Ckc/Ingest) && record.pane_type == PaneType::AtelierEditor. BOTH required: module-context avoids Atelier-as-tab-in-MAIN hiding MAIN tabs [R2]; pane-type avoids stranding Ckc sibling tabs when active [R1].
finalized_approach.P1: Add pub full_window:bool to ModuleDefinition (module_switcher.rs, after default_tab ~:142); true for Ckc(:166-184)+Ingest(:185-196), false for Main/Stage/Lab/Studio. Update drift-gate definitions_match_native_contract (:485-537) in lockstep if it serializes the new field. In BOTH render sites (docked app.rs ~7383-7437 AND popout render_popout_body app.rs ~7717-7775): when predicate true set header_h=0 + tab_h=0 and wrap PaneHeader::show + TabBar::show in if !full_window_module; body gets the full rect.
finalized_approach.P2: DO NOT trim MODULE_DEFINITIONS (avoids orphaning code-symbol/source-control whose only home is Ckc [R3], and drift-gate/persistence churn). The internal [CKC|Posekit|Ingest] atelier strip becomes the only visible tab bar when Atelier is the active full-window surface. Keep current tab order.
finalized_approach.P3: Wire set_module (app.rs ~:4569) -> atelier_panel set_active_tab (atelier_panel.rs:4750): ModuleId::Ckc->CastkitCodex, ModuleId::Ingest->Ingest. Fixes module-ingest-shows-CKC bug (manual_content_editors.rs:497-499). D2=Option A: keep BOTH module ids, MODULE_DEFINITIONS untouched.
finalized_approach.tests: Add negative-chrome assertions to test_ckc_embed.rs ac6_atelier_side_panel_mounted_in_live_shell (~:707): pane-pane-a-header/-title/-lock, tabbar-pane-a, tab-pane-a-0 ABSENT. Add module-ingest->Ingest landing test. Add R1 (open non-Atelier tab in full-window Atelier -> chrome reappears) + R2 (Atelier-as-tab-in-MAIN keeps MAIN chrome) regressions. VERIFY current status of test_accesskit_ids.rs (:125,:368) which hardcode 4-pane chrome the single-pane render never emits; reconcile to the actual single rendered pane, do NOT assume.
finalized_approach.argus_proof: Full-window Atelier is Argus-inspectable/steerable (argus.inspect/click on atelier-tab-* proven by test_ckc_embed.rs:713). Run it as evidence. Navigation contract: CKC module non-Atelier tabs (kernel-dcc/code-symbol/source-control) reachable via module switcher, not the suppressed pane tab bar.
finalized_approach.user_manual: Update in-product UserManual Atelier topic (manual_content_editors.rs) to reflect full-window module (no pane chrome), CKC/PoseKit/Ingest sub-module tabs, module-ckc->CKC / module-ingest->Ingest, navigation contract. HBR-MAN-001/004.
finalized_approach.hbr_int_009_posture: DEFERRED-with-reason: Atelier emits AccessKit/Argus nodes not Flight Recorder; FR round-trip is a verified backend blocker; internal_diagnostics/Palmistry are global/process-level and cover Atelier. PRESERVE the existing FR seam route_to_stage (stage_interop.rs:59-60).

deferrals.R4_persist_internal_tab: Internal atelier active_tab not persisted (singleton AtelierPanelState default CastkitCodex). Address in-MT if cheap, else new MT.
deferrals.R5_swarm_per_pane_tab: Singleton factory shares one internal tab across pop-outs/parallel agents (last-writer-wins). -> existing MT-022 (parallel-agent safe + Argus steerable).
deferrals.quick_switcher_doc_fix: quick_switcher.rs:182 stale doc (Document->AtelierEditor; live opens LoomWikiPage) - minor in-scope cleanup.
deferrals.MT_041_argus_expansion: Menu leaves are open-only dynamic (top_menu_bar.rs:78-82). MT-041 needs leaf-id discovery registry (menu.<x>.<leaf>), two-step open-then-steer Argus protocol, and a click-to-open-persistence proof. Top-level menu-* ids already Argus-visible.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: If a no-context model resumes this MT, first check MT-005 is completed or explicitly waived. Then inspect the live native GUI and AccessKit snapshots for the Atelier pane and its internal tabs before claiming acceptance.

scope.summary: Make the visible Atelier work surface a central, filling pane with internal sibling tabs for Castkit Codex, Posekit, and Ingest. Reuse current native widgets where possible and add stable model-facing IDs for all tabs and content regions.
base_scope: Replace the old right-edge Atelier/CKC side-panel role with a main-pane Atelier surface for `PaneType::AtelierEditor`.
Render internal tabs inside the Atelier pane: `Castkit Codex`, `Posekit`, and `Ingest`.
Make CKC, Posekit, and Ingest siblings so more than one can be represented and addressed by parallel agents.
Reuse the existing `AtelierSidePanel` list/drag-source behavior inside the CKC tab instead of deleting it.
Provide visible placeholder/preview surfaces for Posekit and Ingest if full behavior is not implemented in this MT, but make them stable, nonblank, and model-addressable.
Remove or disable the duplicate right-edge Atelier side panel if it makes the main Atelier panel confusing or steals layout width.

Remove the four-window grid interpretation from Atelier. Atelier is one filling panel with internal CKC, Posekit, and Ingest tabs; multi-window/file/tab behavior belongs to Notes/workbench surfaces, not Atelier.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking the visible Atelier top-level module opens a main filling pane whose active tab is Castkit Codex/CKC.
The Atelier pane contains sibling tabs for Castkit Codex, Posekit, and Ingest.
Each internal tab has a stable AccessKit author_id and exposes a click action.
Each internal tab content region has a stable AccessKit author_id and is nonblank.
The CKC tab reuses current native Atelier/CKC list and drag-source behavior.
Posekit and Ingest tabs are visibly present and usable as starting surfaces even if advanced behavior remains deferred.
Parallel agents can address the top-level module button, Atelier pane, internal tabs, and content regions through stable IDs or backend/debug state.
Visual inspection confirms the main panel fills the work area and the old side-panel layout is not the primary CKC home.
proof_targets: Focused native test proves the Atelier panel internal tabs render with stable IDs.
Focused native interaction test clicks Posekit/Ingest tabs and verifies active content switches.
Live GUI screenshot or visual inspection verifies the top overview, tabs, and content are visible.
AccessKit snapshot includes module-ckc, the Atelier pane, atelier-tab-ckc, atelier-tab-posekit, atelier-tab-ingest, and their content regions.

implementation_plan: 1. Add an `atelier_panel` native module with an internal tab enum/state, stable AccessKit IDs, and visible nonblank content for CKC/Posekit/Ingest.
2. Thread one `AtelierPanel` instance into `HandshakeApp` and use it as the `PaneType::AtelierEditor` factory.
3. Reuse the existing `AtelierSidePanel` widget inside the CKC tab.
4. Update module/tab definitions only where needed so the top-level button remains Atelier and the main pane default tab remains CKC.
5. Add focused tests using existing kittest/AccessKit harnesses.
6. Build, run, and visually inspect the native GUI through the current-source executable.
validation_commands: Launch native GUI and visually inspect Atelier overview/tabs/content

commands_run.evidence: Failed before implementation because exported Posekit/Ingest control author_id constants were missing.
Failed before implementation because live-shell steering did not switch from CKC to Posekit content.
Failed before implementation because the Atelier Tools manual topic was missing.
3 passed, 0 failed. Proves panel tabs and Posekit/Ingest control author_ids/actions.
3 passed, 0 failed. Proves real shell CKC presence and MCP-driven Posekit/Ingest tab switching.
2 passed, 0 failed. Proves Argus and Atelier manual coverage.
3 passed, 0 failed; real-shell CKC presence and MCP-driven Posekit/Ingest tab switching.
2 passed, 0 failed; Argus and Atelier manual coverage.

results.summary: MT-006 remediation implemented for review findings: Posekit/Ingest controls have stable author_ids/actions, real-shell MCP tab switching proof passes, and internal UserManual covers Atelier/Argus navigation. Post-implementation adversarial review is still pending.
Post-review findings remediated; Goodall/Euler review concerns reduced to runtime proof gap, then targeted runtime proofs passed in this session.

risk_notes.risk: The new main panel duplicates the old side panel and makes navigation more confusing.
risk_notes.failure_scenario: The operator sees two CKC/Atelier homes and cannot tell which is canonical.
risk_notes.mitigation: Make the main filling panel canonical in this MT and remove or keep closed the duplicate side panel unless explicitly needed as a secondary drag source.

risk_notes.risk: Posekit and Ingest become dead labels.
risk_notes.failure_scenario: Tabs are visible but expose no stable region, no status, and no future implementation anchor.
risk_notes.mitigation: Each tab must render a nonblank structured surface with stable IDs and explicit current capabilities/deferred gaps.

risk_notes.risk: Parallel agents cannot operate the panel reliably.
risk_notes.failure_scenario: Tabs can only be found by screen text or pixel position.
risk_notes.mitigation: Add stable AccessKit author_ids for panel, tabs, and content, and verify them in tests.

review_lenses: operator GUI shape and layout confusion

manual_requirement: Update the in-product internal UserManual in the same implementation change so operators and models know Atelier is the main filling panel and CKC, Posekit, and Ingest are sibling tabs.

handoff.summary: Implemented: Atelier is a single filling panel with CKC, Posekit, and Ingest sibling tabs; Posekit/Ingest controls are model-addressable; real-shell MCP tab switching and manual coverage pass. Awaiting validator.

risks: A visual restructure can break existing CKC drag payload behavior.
A pane-local tab system can conflict with the shell's outer tab bar.
A visible placeholder can be mistaken for implemented Posekit/Ingest behavior.
minimum_controls: Reuse the existing CKC widget for CKC content.
Keep internal tab IDs namespaced under atelier.*.
Make any deferred behavior explicit in content and tests without claiming full parity.

findings: Stale visual/MCP proof still targets old pane-b/four-pane path instead of module-ckc -> atelier-tab-* in the single-pane Atelier model.
Posekit and Ingest visible controls lack stable author_id/action coverage beyond tab selection, so parallel agents cannot reliably steer them.
Real HandshakeApp shell coverage proves CKC only; it does not prove Posekit/Ingest tab switching and content visibility through the live shell path.
required_remediation: Update test_visual_interaction_proof.rs to steer module-ckc, atelier-tab-posekit, and atelier-tab-ingest through click_widget or argus.click and assert content IDs.
Add stable author_ids/actions for Posekit yaw/reset/marker controls and Ingest pass/reject/unsure/tag controls.
Add a real-shell test beside the CKC live-shell proof for Posekit/Ingest tab switching.
adversarial_review.summary: Post-review remediation added stable Posekit/Ingest controls and live-shell tab switching proof; targeted tests passed.
